perturbation.original_text: The term of this Agreement shall commence as of the Effective Date set f orth below and shall continue for a period of twelve (12) months thereafter.
perturbation.changed_text: The term of this Agreement shall commence as of the Effective Date set f orth below and shall continue for a period of six (6) months thereafter.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by shortening the agreement term from twelve months to six months. This directly contradicts the original intent and creates uncertainty regarding the confidentiality period and its enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Scope and Term of Agreement. This Agreement pertains to all Confidential Information that is disclosed o r otherwise made available by the Disclosing Party or its Affiliates to the Receiving Party in connection with the NDA Purpose, whether prior to or during the term of this Agreement. The term of this Agreement shall commence as of the Effective Date set f orth below and shall continue for a period of six (6) months thereafter.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the term of the agreement from twelve months to six months. The contradicted text states that 'The term of this Agreement shall commence as of the Effective Date set forth below and shall continue for a period of six (6) months thereafter.' While the contradicted text aligns with the changed text, the initial agreement of twelve (12) months contradicts with the new six (6) months. Therefore there is in-text contradiction, satisfying the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party shall, upon the written request of the Disclosing Party, return to the Disclos ing Party or, at the Disclosing Party’s direction, destroy all documents and any and all other tangible manifestations of Confidential Information received or created by the Receiving Party pursuant to this Agreement (and all copies and reproductions there of).
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party may, at its sole discretion, return or destroy all documents and any and all other tangible manifestations of Confidential Information received or created by the Receiving Party pursuant to this Agreement (and all copies and reproductions there of).
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by changing the obligation of the Receiving Party to return or destroy confidential information upon the Disclosing Party's request to a discretionary action. The original text mandates return/destruction upon request, whereas the changed text gives the Receiving Party the option, causing uncertainty about the Receiving Party's duty regarding Confidential Information and potential disputes about compliance.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Disclosure and Protection of Confidential Information. The Receiving Party shall (i) hold all Confidential Information in confidence, and shall not disclose Confidential Information except as expressly permitted under the terms of this Agreement and (ii) use Confidential Information only for the NDA Purpose and for no other purpose without the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the Receiving Party's obligation regarding confidential information. Originally, the Receiving Party *shall* return or destroy confidential information upon the Disclosing Party's request, as also indicated by the Contradicted Text. However, the changed text states that the Receiving Party *may*, at its sole discretion, return or destroy the information. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states 'The Receiving Party shall (i) hold all Confidential Information in confidence... and (ii) use Confidential Information only for the NDA Purpose...' By making the return/destruction of confidential information discretionary, it weakens the protection and use of the Confidential Information, potentially violating the Receiving Party's duty to hold it in confidence as mandated by the Contradicted Text. Therefore, it's a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The obligations of the Receiving Party with respect to the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information shall not apply with respect to any Confidential Information to the extent the Receiving Party can demonstrate that such Confidential Information: (a) is generally known to the public at the time of disclosure or becomes generally known through no breach of this Agreement; (b) is in the Receiving Party’s rightful possession at the time of disclosure without an obligation to hold it in Page 2 of 2 confidence; (c) becomes known to the Receiving Party without obligation of confidence through disclosure by a third party h aving the legal right to disclose such information; (d) is independently developed by the Receiving Party without access or reference to, or reliance upon, the Confidential Information ; or (e) is or becomes disclosed through the ordinary course of the NDA Purpose or as an element of any marketing plan intended to be released in the public domain or to targeted geographic public domain .
perturbation.changed_text: The obligations of the Receiving Party with respect to the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information shall not apply with respect to any Confidential Information without limitations. The Receiving Party will not be held responsible of the Confidential Information
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by completely removing all limitations to the Receiving Party's obligations related to Confidential Information. The original text provided specific exceptions where the Receiving Party's obligations would not apply, such as information already publicly known. The changed text eliminates all such exceptions, creating conflict and undermining the protections for Confidential Information outlined elsewhere in the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Disclosure and Protection of Confidential Information. The Receiving Party shall (i) hold all Confidential Information in confidence, and shall not disclose Confidential Information except as expressly permitted under the terms of this Agreement and (ii) use Confidential Information only for the NDA Purpose and for no other purpose without the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the Receiving Party has no limitations regarding Confidential Information and will not be held responsible for it. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which states that the Receiving Party 'shall hold all Confidential Information in confidence' and 'shall not disclose Confidential Information except as expressly permitted under the terms of this Agreement'. The changed text removes all obligations, while the contradicted text imposes specific obligations, creating a clear and direct conflict and a strong in-text contradiction, as per the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: AGX and CANDIDATE desire to exchange information and to engage in discussions concerning AGX’s agent program and Candidate’s qualifications to establish a freight Agency Relationship. In the course of such discussions and negotiations, it is anticipated that a Party (“Disclosing Party”) may disclose or otherwise make available to the other Party (“Receiving Party”) certain confidential and proprietary inform ation for the purpose of enabling the Receiving Party to evaluate and the prospective business relationship (the “NDA Purpose”).
perturbation.changed_text: AGX and CANDIDATE desire to exchange information and to engage in discussions concerning AGX’s agent program and Candidate’s qualifications to establish a freight Agency Relationship. In the course of such discussions and negotiations, all information exchanged will be considered public knowledge.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction. It claims that all information exchanged is considered public knowledge, which directly contradicts the fundamental purpose of the NDA which is to maintain confidentiality. This creates conflict and renders the entire agreement regarding Confidential Information useless.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information. As used in this Agreement, the term “Confidential Information” shall mean all information that is not generally known or readily ascertainable by proper means by other persons and is either (i) des ignated as “confidential” or “proprietary” by the Disclosing Party prior to or at the time of disclosure or (ii) information that a reasonable person should recognize as being confidential or proprietary, based on the nature of the information and/or the c ircumstances of its disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states "all information exchanged will be considered public knowledge." This directly contradicts the "Confidential Information" section, which defines what constitutes confidential information and establishes its protected status. The original text set the stage for confidential exchange of information under the NDA, while the changed text negates any confidentiality.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement pertains to all Confidential Information that is disclosed o r otherwise made available by the Disclosing Party or its Affiliates to the Receiving Party in connection with the NDA Purpose, whether prior to or during the term of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement pertains only to Confidential Information that is disclosed in writing by the Disclosing Party to the Receiving Party after the execution of this Agreement. Any prior or verbal disclosures are excluded.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction by limiting the scope of the NDA. The original text covered all confidential information disclosed prior to *or* during the agreement. The change limits the scope to only written information disclosed *after* the agreement. This introduces uncertainty and conflicts with the intention of the NDA to protect pre-existing confidential information discussed during initial negotiations, by exluding prior and verbal disclosures, creating ambiguity about the Receiving Party obligations regarding any pre-existing confidental material shared
perturbation.contradicted_text: Background. AGX and CANDIDATE desire to exchange information and to engage in discussions concerning AGX’s agent program and Candidate’s qualifications to establish a freight Agency Relationship. In the course of such discussions and negotiations, it is anticipated that a Party (“Disclosing Party”) may disclose or otherwise make available to the other Party (“Receiving Party”) certain confidential and proprietary inform ation for the purpose of enabling the Receiving Party to evaluate and the prospective business relationship (the “NDA Purpose”).
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text encompasses all confidential information exchanged prior to or during the agreement. The changed text narrows the scope to only written confidential information disclosed *after* the agreement's execution. This directly conflicts with the contradicted text in the 'Background' section, which states that AGX and CANDIDATE desire to exchange information during discussions and negotiations, which anticipates prior disclosures. By excluding prior disclosures, the changed text creates a clear inconsistency, making it a strong in-text contradiction as per the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.
